lessonHubModel.title: The Mice Who Lived in a Shoe
dailyFocus: Vocabulary: admired, fetched, gathered, huddled, merchant, observed, platform, safety, shelter, timber
Grammar / GUM: Common and Proper Nouns

ela: 2.RL.1 Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text.
2.RL.3 Describe how characters respond to major events and challenges.
2.RF.3 Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills.
2.L.1 Demonstrate command of conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
wida: ELP Standard 1: Social and Instructional Language
ELP Standard 2: Language for Language Arts

mrsParrishRecommends: Teach vocabulary before reading so students are not stopped by unfamiliar words.
Spend extra time with merchant because many students do not use that word in daily speech.
Use partner talk before answering cause-and-effect questions.
Do not skip the skills practice pages; they reinforce what the assessment expects.
teacherBrain: Open Court packets are valuable because they hit skills and topics that may otherwise be missed.
Use this lesson to model how characters respond to a problem.
Good place to introduce a cause/effect anchor chart.
ufliSupport: Use UFLI Toolbox as needed for digraph review.
Use blending board for /ch/, /th/, /sh/, and /wh/ review.
Use Elkonin boxes for students needing phoneme support.
